text:
## Report

Based on analyzing all open pull requests across all repositories:

**Repository Breakdown:**
- `acme/api`: 1 PR
- `acme/infra`: 3 PRs  
- `acme/web`: 1 PR

**Total Count:** 5 open pull requests with more than 400 additions that do not close any Linear issue.

**PR with Most Additions:** #1034 (with 603 additions in the `acme/web` repository)

text: Now let me search for Linear-related functions to understand how to check if a PR closes a Linear issue

data: [{"name":"linear__list_issues","description":"List Linear issues, optionally filtered by assignee, state, team, or project.","args":[":assignee?",":state?",":team?",":project?"],"effect":"read"},{"name":"linear__get_issue","description":"Get one Linear issue by its id (e.g. ENG-123).","args":[":id"]…(+1162)

text: Let me also search for how PRs might link to Linear issues

data: [{"name":"github__list_pull_requests","description":"List pull requests, optionally filtered by repo, state (open/merged/closed), or author.","args":[":repo?",":state?",":author?"],"effect":"read"},{"name":"github__get_pull_request","description":"Get a single pull request by repo and number.","args…(+1988)

text: I need to understand the data structure better. Let me first check what information is available in pull requests and then see if there's a way to check for Linear issue references. Let me start by getting all open pull requests across all …(+12)
Now let me examine one PR to understand the data structure and see if there's any information about Linear issues

data: {"value":{"number":1000,"repo":"acme/infra","title":"SSO SCIM provisioning errors: fix sso-provisioning","author":"niaj","state":"open","additions":94,"deletions":234,"created_at":"2026-05-23T00:00:00.000Z","merged_at":null,"base":"main","closes_linear":null,"body":"This change addresses sso scim pr…(+21)

text: Great! I can see there's a `closes_linear` field. Let me check a few more to see if some have Linear issues
Good, so some PRs do have Linear issues. Now let me filter for PRs with more than 400 additions and no Linear issues

data: {"value":5,"defined":["target-prs"],"defs":{"target-prs":{"count":5,"peek":{"number":1020,"repo":"acme/api","title":"Migrate auth to OAuth2 + PKCE: add test …","author":"grace"}}}}

text: Perfect! Now I need to get the count for each repository, the total count, and the PR number of the single such pull request with the most additions. Let me proceed

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":";; Group by repository and count\n(def repo-counts (frequencies (map :repo target-prs)))\nrepo-counts"}

text: Now let me get the total count and find the PR with the most additions

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Total count
(def total-count (count target-prs))

;; Find the PR with maximum additions
(def max-additions-pr (apply max-key :additions target-prs))

{:repo-counts repo-counts
 :total-count total-count
 :max-additions-pr-number (:number max-additions-pr)
 :max-additions (:additions max-additions-pr)
 :max-additions-repo (:repo max-additions-pr)}"}

text: Let me double-check by looking at all the target PRs to verify
